description: During the summer, kidney shaped sporangia (spore-bearing structures) develop in the axils of upper leaves. Sporangia-bearing leaves are slightly smaller than leaves without sporangia.
origin: Central and Eastern Canada, Eastern United States
synonyms: ["Lycopodium selago var. lucidulum", "Lycopodium lucidulum var. verum", "Lycopodium lucidulum var. tryonii", "Urostachys lucidulus var. reclinatus", "Huperzia lucidula var. tryonii", "Urostachys lucidulus", "Huperzia selago subsp. lucidula", "Lycopodium lucidulum"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day) | Climate: temperate

description: Huperzia selago, the northern firmoss or fir clubmoss, is a vascular plant in the family Lycopodiaceae. It is small-ish, sturdy, stiff and upright and densely scale-leaved. This plant is an evergreen, perennial pteridophyte. The spores are produced June to September. It has a circumpolar distribution.
origin: ITA VC, RUW OO, EASTERN CANADA, YAK OO, NORTHEASTERN USA
synonyms: ["Lycopodium selago var. densum", "Huperzia selago var. sulcata", "Lycopodium selago f. patens", "Lycopodium disseminatum", "Urostachys selago var. incurvus", "Urostachys selago f. alpestris", "Lycopodium densum", "Plananthus patens", "Urostachys selago var. groenlandicus", "Lycopodium selago var. laxum"]

care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Parts: Aerial part (or whole plant). (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)

description: Huperzia serrata, the toothed clubmoss, is a plant known as a firmoss. The species is native to eastern Asia. It is also found in the main islands of Hawaii with the exception of Maui, but is considered vulnerable by NatureServe.
origin: MEXICO, NNS, PRM OO, CHM HJ, CUB
synonyms: ["Huperzia serrata f. intermedia", "Lycopodium serratum", "Huperzia serrata var. paucidentata", "Urostachys serratus var. japoniconeotropicus", "Lycopodium sargassifolium", "Urostachys serratus var. sachalinensis", "Lycopodium serratum f. intermedium", "Urostachys serratus", "Huperzia serrata var. intermedia", "Lycopodium serratum var. minor"]

description: Lycopodiella alopecuroides, the foxtail clubmoss, is a species of perennial vascular plant in the club-moss family, Lycopodiaceae. It is commonly found along the Atlantic seaboard and has recently been discovered in the state of Maine. The family, Lycopodiaceae contains nearly 15 genera and about 375 species
origin: SCA OO, MRY OO, MSI OO, NORTHEASTERN USA, AGE CH
synonyms: ["Plananthus alopecuroides", "Lycopodium inundatum var. alopecuroides", "Lycopodium alopecuroides", "Lepidotis alopecuroides"]

description:
Lycopodiella appressa, commonly known as southern bog clubmoss, is a species of clubmoss. It is native to
eastern North America, including Cuba and the West Indies. In the United States, it is primarily found on the Coastal Plain.
origin: Eastern Canada and United States, south to Cuba
synonyms: ["Lycopodium inundatum var. appressum", "Lycopodium alopecuroides f. appressum", "Lycopodium adpressum f. polyclavatum", "Lycopodium alopecuroides var. appressum", "Lycopodium alopecuroides f. polyclavatum", "Lycopodium alopecuroides subsp. appressum", "Lycopodium inundatum f. polyclavatum", "Lycopodiella bigelowii", "Lycopodium alopecuroides var. chapmanii", "Lycopodium appressum"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Partial Shade (Direct sunlight only part of the day, 2-6 hours) | Climate: temperate

description: Palhinhaea cernua, synonym Lycopodiella cernua and Lycopodium cernuum, is a plant in the family Lycopodiaceae, commonly known as the staghorn clubmoss. The Hawaiian name for the plant is wāwaeʻiole, or "rat's foot". It has a substantial number of scientific synonyms in several genera. The genus Palhinhaea is accepted in the Pteridophyte Phylogeny Group classification of 2016, but not in other classifications which submerge the genus in Lycopodiella.
origin: IND KE, EHM AP, NIC, MLI, VAN
synonyms: ["Palhinhaea capillacea", "Palhinhaea polycephala", "Lycopodium cernuum var. watsonianum", "Palhinhaea cernua f. laxa", "Lycopodiella cernua var. sikkimensis", "Palhinhaea cernua f. sikkimensis", "Lycopodium cernuum f. macrostachyum", "Palhinhaea mariana", "Lycopodium cernuum var. laxum", "Lepidotis cernua"]

description: Lycopodiella inundata is a species of club moss known by the common names inundated club moss, marsh clubmoss and northern bog club moss. It has a circumpolar and circumboreal distribution, occurring throughout the northern Northern Hemisphere from the Arctic to montane temperate regions in Eurasia and North America. It grows in wet habitat, such as bogs, ponds, moist spots on the tundra, and long-standing borrow pits.
origin: ITA VC, RUW OO, EASTERN CANADA, NORTHEASTERN USA, NOR OO
synonyms: ["Plananthus inundatus", "Lycopodium inundatum", "Lepidotis inundata", "Lycopodium palustre", "Lepidotis incurva", "Lycopodium inundatum var. typicum", "Lycopodium inundatum var. rigidium", "Lycopodium inundatum var. diversifolium", "Lycopodium inundatum f. furcatum", "Lycopodium inundatum f. funiforme"]

description: Diphasiastrum alpinum, the alpine clubmoss, is a species of clubmoss. This plant is a glaucous scale-leaved perennial pteridophyte. In Finland, the spores are produced June to September. It was first described by Carl Linnaeus in his Flora Lapponica, 1737, from specimens obtained in Finland.
origin: ITA VC, TCS AR, NCS ST, EASTERN CANADA, YAK OO
synonyms: ["Lycopodium alpinum f. umbrosum", "Diphasiastrum kablikianum", "Lycopodium alpinum var. sabinifolium", "Lycopodium alpinum subsp. cupressifolium", "Lycopodium alpinum subsp. kablikianum", "Stachygynandrum alpinum", "Diphasium alpinum", "Lycopodium complanatum subsp. alpinum", "Diphasiastrum alpinum var. ergakense", "Lycopodium cupressifolium"]

ja: チシマヒカゲノカズラ

description: Spinulum annotinum, synonym Lycopodium annotinum, known as interrupted club-moss, or stiff clubmoss, is a species of clubmoss native to forests of the colder parts of North America, as well as Asia, and most of Europe. It is an evergreen perennial pteridophyte. The genus Spinulum is accepted in the Pteridophyte Phylogeny Group classification of 2016, but not in other classifications, which submerge the genus in Lycopodium.
origin: VER OO, MAS, ABT, TEN OO, BLT LA
synonyms: ["Lycopodium annotinum var. subpungens", "Lycopodium bryophyllum", "Lycopodium annotinum f. groenlandicum", "Lepidotis annotina", "Lycopodium annotinum f. angustatum", "Lycopodium annotinum f. proliferum", "Lycopodium juniperifolium", "Lycopodium annotinum var. angustatum", "Lycopodium annotinum var. acrifolium", "Lycopodium annotinum var. latifolium"]

care.toxicity_note: Human: weak toxic. Parts: Whole plant. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)

description: Lycopodium clavatum is the most widespread species in the genus Lycopodium in the clubmoss family.
origin: Alaska, Canada, south to Georgia and west to California.

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight) | Climate: temperate
care.toxicity_note: Human: toxic. Parts: Whole plant. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
care.used_for: ["Spores have been used as a powder for infants and to protect raw skin. It has been used to treat unspecified medicinal disorders", "as animal food", "an ornamental plant and a medicine."]
care.used_for_details: Spores have been used as a powder for infants and to protect raw skin. It has been used to treat unspecified medicinal disorders, as animal food, an ornamental plant and a medicine.

description: Diphasiastrum complanatum, common names groundcedar, creeping jenny, or northern running-pine, is a species of clubmoss native to dry coniferous forests in colder northerly parts of the world. Under the original name Lycopodium complanatum, this was an inclusive superspecies that included a number of other species now known to be biologically separate. This plant is an evergreen, perennial pteridophyte. The spores are produced June to September.
origin: IND KE, EHM AP, VER OO, ABT, BLT LA
synonyms: ["Diphasiastrum wilceae", "Lycopodium complanatum subvar. proliferum", "Lycopodium complanatum f. simplex", "Lycopodium complanatum f. brachypodum", "Lycopodium complanatum subvar. recurvatum", "Diphasiastrum montellii", "Lepidotis complanata", "Diphasiastrum hastulatum", "Lycopodium complanatum var. flabellatum", "Diphasiastrum complanatum f. montellii"]

description: Lycopodium japonicum is a common species of plant in the genus Lycopodium in the clubmoss family. It is widespread in China, Japan and countries of Southern Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for the treatment of sprains, strains and myasthenia, and research is ongoing into its efficacy. A type of alkaloid with an unusual structure, labelled lycojaponicumins A through E, were isolated from the plant in 2012.
origin: CBD OO, INDIAN SUBCONTINENT, NNS OO, IND WB, CHS SH
synonyms: ["Lycopodium divaricatum", "Lycopodium japonicum var. wallichianum", "Lycopodium pseudoclavatum var. yunnanense", "Lycopodium japonicum f. yunnanense", "Lycopodium centrochinense", "Lycopodium interjectum", "Stachygynandrum japonicum", "Lycopodium pseudoclavatum", "Lycopodium kinabaluense", "Lycopodium clavatum var. nipponicum"]

description: Lycopodium lagopus, commonly known as one-cone club-moss, is an arctic and subarctic species of plants in the genus Lycopodium in the clubmoss family. It is widespread in cold, northerly regions: Canada, Greenland, Russia, Scandinavia, and the northern United States including Alaska.
origin: MAG, VER OO, MAS, NUN, ABT
synonyms: ["Lycopodium clavatum var. brevispicatum", "Lycopodium clavatum var. lagopus", "Lycopodium clavatum subsp. megastachyon", "Lycopodium clavatum f. furcatum", "Lycopodium clavatum var. megastachyon", "Lycopodium integrifolium", "Lycopodium clavatum var. integerrimum", "Lycopodium clavatum var. integrifolium", "Lycopodium clavatum subsp. monostachyon", "Lycopodium clavatum var. monostachyon"]
